The fact that there is no checking account correlates with decreased default likelihood, because it correlates with decreased default likelihood, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that a critical existing credit history can still be classified into the good class, because it can still be classified into the good class, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that homeownership exhibits a weaker statistical association with bad credit risk, because it exhibits a weaker statistical association with bad credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
